Crotalaria crotalarioides, also known as Earleaf Crotalaria, is a plant species in the legume family (Fabaceae) that is native to the Americas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include the potential to treat various health issues such as fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties.

The bioactive constituents of Crotalaria crotalarioides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

Crotalaria crotalarioides is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions. However, it's essential to note that the use of this plant for medicinal purposes should be approached with caution, as it may interact with certain medications or exacerbate underlying health conditions.
